abstract.para: The cluster automatically restarts resources if they fail, but each failure increases the resource's fail count. If the fail count reaches the resource's migration threshold, the current node becomes ineligible to run the resource. The cluster either moves the resource to an eligible node or stops the resource if no eligible node is available.
By default, fail counts are not automatically reset. This procedure explains how to reset the fail count manually.

topic.para: You can clean up failed resources with the &crmshell; (&crmsh;) or with the &hawk; Web interface. If the resource is part of a group, the other resources in the group are also cleaned up.

step.para: Log in either as the &rootuser; user or as a user with sudo privileges.
Show the status of the cluster, including fail counts:
If a resource has a fail count, it appears in the Migration Summary section.
Clean up the resource on all nodes:
Alternatively, you can clean up the resource on a specific node:
In a Web browser, go to https://HAWKSERVER:7630/ and log in.
From the left navigation bar, select Monitoring Status.
Find the resource you want to clean up. In its Operations column, click the down arrow icon and select Cleanup.
From the drop-down list, either keep the default Clean up on all nodes or select a specific node.
A message at the top of the screen shows if the action was successful.

tip.title: Automatically resetting fail counts
tip.para: You can configure a cluster resource's fail count to reset automatically by adding the failure-timeout meta attribute to the resource.
